name: Login Disconnect
desc: Kicks the client during the login phase with a JSON chat component reason message (e.g. 'You are banned', 'Server is full').

fields.name: reason
fields.desc: The disconnect reason message displayed to the player

encoding: Serialized as JSON chat component string

implementation.overview: S00PacketDisconnect is the login-phase equivalent of S40PacketDisconnect (play phase). The server sends this when it rejects the login for any reason: banned, whitelisted, server full, outdated client, invalid session, VPN detected, etc. The packet contains a single JSON ChatComponent that is displayed to the player on the disconnect screen. Modules target this for: AutoReconnect (detecting the kick reason and immediately reconnecting), KickMessageReader (extracting ban reasons), and AntiKick (cancelling the packet entirely to stay connected — known as 'ghost mode' where the client stays on the server despite being kicked).

modules.name: AutoReconnect / AntiKick
modules.purpose: Automatically reconnect to the server when kicked during login, or cancel the kick packet to stay connected in a 'ghost' state where the server thinks you're disconnected but you're still on.
modules.how_it_works: Approach 1 (AutoReconnect): The module reads the disconnect reason from the ChatComponent, then automatically initiates a new connection to the same server. Some versions filter the reason — if it's a ban, it won't reconnect; if it's 'Server full', it will keep retrying. Approach 2 (AntiKick/GhostMode): The module cancels S00PacketDisconnect and S40PacketDisconnect, then continues sending/receiving packets as normal. The server has already marked the player as disconnected and released their resources, but the client can still observe the world (ghost mode).
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleDisconnect(S00PacketDisconnect) — called when the server sends a disconnect during login. The vanilla behavior immediately closes the NetworkManager channel and displays the reason on the disconnect screen.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: GhostMode (cancelling disconnect packets) is very detectable: the server has already removed the player from the player list, unloaded their chunks, and released entity IDs. Any subsequent packets from the ghost client will appear as packets from an unknown entity and be immediately rejected. Anti-cheats that track entity IDs in the player list will flag ghost clients.

modules.name: BypassDetector / VPN Check Bypass
modules.purpose: Read the disconnect reason to detect why you were kicked (VPN detection, ban, server full) and adapt the next connection attempt (e.g., switch VPN, clear HWID).
modules.how_it_works: The module intercepts the ChatComponent reason, parses the JSON, and looks for keywords like 'VPN', 'proxy', 'banned', 'blacklisted'. Based on the reason, it can take automated action: switch to a different VPN/proxy, clear client-side HWID stored values, change the connecting IP, or delay the reconnect to avoid rate limiting.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleDisconnect(S00PacketDisconnect) — the reason field is an IChatComponent that can contain styled text and click events.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Servers sometimes add fake/misleading disconnect reasons to confuse automated tools. For example, a server might say 'Banned' when it's actually a rate-limit. Anti-VPN plugins also add random delays to disconnect to prevent automated retry loops.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client-side: NetHandlerLoginClient.handleDisconnect(S00PacketDisconnect) receives this during login phase. The handler calls netManager.closeChannel(reason) which displays the disconnect screen.

Server-side: This is sent by the server during the NetHandlerLoginServer processing (e.g., after failed auth, banned player check, whitelist check). The server uses ServerConfigurationManager to validate the player before sending login success.
implementation.client_variations:
Gugustus: The AntiKick module cancels S00PacketDisconnect entirely to enter ghost mode — the client never closes the connection.
Rise 6.0: BlinkComponent exempts S00PacketDisconnect from blink (it must be processed immediately to avoid desync).
Jigsaw 0.26: GhostMode module hooks the handleDisconnect method to prevent the network close.
RektSky B5: Disabler module has a specific mode for cancelling disconnect packets on certain servers.
